titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD55/69

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=834 status=reviewed modified=2023-03-13 15:02:37.150772+00 | translation moa=835 status=reviewed modified=2023-03-13 15:02:37.136271+00

div.p:
Omnibus Sancte matris ecclesie filiis . tam presentibus quam futuris . Ricardus le Walais . Salutem . Sciatis me dedisse . et hac mea carta confirmasse deo et ecclesie Sancte marie de
Meylros . et monachis ibidem deo seruientibus pro animabus dauid et Melcolmi . Regum Scottorum . et Comitis Henrici . et Walteri filii Alani domini mei . et omnium antecessorum . et successorum eorum
et pro animabus patris et matris mee . et omnium parentum . et antecessorum et successorum meorum . et pro salute domini mei . W . Regis Scottorum . et comitis dauid fratris eius . et alani filii Walteri domini
mei . et omnium antecessorum et successorum eius . et pro salute mea . et uxoris mee . et omnium heredum nostrorum . illam terra in Galwedia que uocatur Godenehc . per has diuisas . scilicet a capite
burne que uocatur polncarn . usque ad quercum in qua crux facta est . et sic sursum uersus septentrionem per quendam sicum . qui est iuxta predictum quercum . uersus orientem . usque ad loudonetha
et sic in transuersum musse uersus occidentem . per quendam sicum . usque . ad caput burne que cadit in saxnoc ad clonsangan . Dedi etiam predicte ecclesie illam terram que uocatur baremor . per
has diuisas . scilicet ab hostio burne de hactenewetne sursum usque ad caput eiusdem burne . et sic a capite burne sursum per quoddam uetus fossatum . usque . ad bog sub
hactnedonan . et sic deorsum sicut cruces facte sunt in quercus usque . ad louteuenan . et sic sursum uersus septemtrionem per uetus fossatum . usque . ad polnecreihs . et sic deorsum
sicut polnecreihs descendit in fale . et sic deorsum per fale . usque . ad hostium burne de hactnewetne . Totas has terras . et quicquid continetur infra prescriptas diuisas dedi
ecclesie de Meylros . in liberam . et puram . et perpetuam elemosinam in perpetuum tenendas de me et heredibus meis . pro . ii . Marcis argenti ad duos terminos . mihi et heredibus meis
annuatim redendis . scilicet ad penthecosten . i . marcam . et unam ad festum sancti Martini . ita libere . quiete . plene . et honorifice . sicut ullam elemosinam liberius . quietius . plenius
et honorificentius tenent et possident . sine omni terreno seruitio . et consuetudine . et exactione seculari . salua mihi et heredibus meis prefata pensione . Et ego et
heredes mei manutenebimus . et warantizabimus eis prefatas terras . contra omnes homines in perpetuum . et adquietabimus . de forensi . et omni terreno seruitio uersus dominum regem et
omnes dominos nostros . saluis cartis eorum de Machel . quas habent de Waltero filio alani . et Alano filio Walteri dominis meis . et quietis clamatis de me et heredibus meis in perpetuum .
His . Testibus . I . Glasg’ episcopo . E . Abbate de dunfermelino . R . abbate de kinlos . A . abbate de cupro . Willelmo maluaisin archidicaono de laodonie . Hugone capellano regis . Roberto filio Roberti . Werneb’

p.cl: To all sons of Holy Mother Church, as well present as to come, Richard Wallace sends greeting.

Know me to have given, and by this my charter to have made firm, to God and to the church of Saint Mary at Melrose, and to the monks there serving God, for the souls of David and Malcolm, kings of the Scots, and of Earl Henry, and of Walter fitz Alan, my lord, and of all their ancestors and successors; and for the souls of my father and my mother, and of all my forefathers, and my ancestors and successors; and for the salvation of my lord, William, king of the Scots, and of Earl David his brother, and of Alan fitz Walter my lord, and of all his ancestors and successors, and for my own salvation, and my wife's, and all our heirs'; that land in Galloway which is called 'Godenehc'; by these boundaries, to wit, from the head of the burn which is called 'Polncarn', as far as the oak tree on which a cross has been made, and so up, northwards by a syke which is near the said oak tree, towards the east, as far as 'Loudonetha', and so across the bog towards the west along a syke, as far as the head of the burn which falls into the 'Saxnoc' at 'Clonsangan'. I have also given to the said church the land which is called Barmuir, by these boundaries, to wit, from the mouth of the burne at 'Hactenewetne' upwards as fas as the head of the same burn, and so from the head of the burn upwards along an old ditch as far as the bog below 'Hactnedonan', and so downwards as the crosses have been made on the oak trees, as far as 'Louteuenan, and so up, northwards by the old ditch as far as 'Polnecreihs', and so downwards as 'Polnecreihs' descends into the 'Fale', and so downwards along the 'Fale', as far as the mouth of the burn at 'Hactnewetne'. All these lands and whatever is contained within the afore-written bounds have I given to the church of Melrose, in free, pure, and perpetual alms for ever, to be held of me and my heirs in return for 2 merks of silver to be rendered to me and my heirs at two terms, to wit, one merk at Pentecost and one at the feast of Saint Martin; so freely, quitly, fully, and honourably as hey hold and possess any alms freely, quitly, fully, and honourably, without every earthly service, custom and secular exaction; reserving to myself and my heirs the aforementioned pension. And I and my heirs shall maintain and warrant to them the aforementioned lands against all men for ever, and acquit from forinsec and every earthly service towards the lord king and all our lords, except for their charters concerning Mauchline which they have from Walter fitz Alan and Alan fitz Walter my lords, and quitclaimed for me and my heirs for ever.

With these witnesses, Jocelin, bishop of Glasgow, Archibald abbot of Dunfermline, Ralph abbot of Kinloss, Adam abbot of Cupar, William Malveisin, archdeacon of Lothian, Hugh the king's chaplain, Robert son of Robert Warnebald.
